project.name: Knotz Raven Mayhem
project.productGoal: Prepare Knotz Raven Mayhem for public release. Core gameplay, progression, staged run flow, cabinet demo screens, performance pooling, and release verification scripts are in place; the next team should focus on SEO, web kit/release shell polish, final release QA, and GitHub Pages deployment validation.
project.v1ScopeDecision: Local records and local progression only. Online leaderboards and account features are deferred beyond the first public release.

currentState.summary: The original raven click-target prototype has been rebuilt into a Phaser 3 + TypeScript + Vite arcade shooter with staged runs, bosses, bonus round pacing, between-stage interstitials, armory-forward game over, armory progression, persistent local saves, DOM overlays, idle cabinet demo/tutorial screens, procedural stage art, stage-aware procedural audio, pooled transient effects, release verification scripts, and GitHub Actions workflow scaffolding.

implementedContent.stages: 9 named stages with different spawn pools, palettes, speed multipliers, rewards, procedural set dressing, a Jackpot Alley bonus round, overflow scaling after the final named stage, and field-drain stage transitions that wait for active ravens and powerups before showing stage clear.
implementedContent.enemies: 11 enemy definitions: normal, fast, golden, armored, mini, shield, splitter, dive, wraith, brute, and boss. Stage pools now reveal enemy types gradually across the full 9-stage arc instead of front-loading most variants early.
implementedContent.weapons: 4 guns: Quarter Pistol, Burst Rifle, Scattergun, Arc Laser.
implementedContent.assistChips: 4 Assist Chip unlocks: Stock Cabinet, Turbo Capacitor, Threat Scanner, Chaos Net. These are cabinet hardware purchases that tune recharge feel and target forgiveness while the live reticle identity follows the equipped gun.
implementedContent.upgrades: 4 permanent upgrade tracks: cooldown, combo window, starting lives, payout scaling.
implementedContent.powerups: 6 powerups: slow-mo, multi-shot, score boost, extra life, overdrive, coin rush.
implementedContent.audio: Procedural WebAudio with menu/run/boss modes, stage-aware motifs, weapon-specific shots, enemy-specific hit cues, powerup cues, boss warning/defeat stings, stage clear, miss, and game over.
implementedContent.visuals: Seed raven/explosion assets plus procedural stage set dressing, mobile particle caps, stronger death sequence, cabinet-style HUD framing, weapon-driven reticles, Assist Chip effect labels, cooldown feedback, projectile traces, multishot chain traces, shotgun spread visualization, laser lane feedback, and pooled transient effects for long-session performance.
implementedContent.releaseOps: Release verification scripts, balance report, served dist smoke, GitHub Actions CI, and manual GitHub Pages workflow are in place.

latestPlaytestUpdate.summary: Recent playtests have been working well. The between-stage screens, game-over push toward the armory, cabinet demo/tutorial screens, staggered raven reveal curve, field-drain stage endings, and performance pooling pass should be treated as accepted baseline behavior unless new bugs appear.
validatedSignals: Between-stage interstitials are no longer a theory item; they are working well in playtests.
Game-over-to-armory flow makes sense to players and supports the intended progression loop.
The latest gameplay-flow changes should be treated as accepted baseline behavior unless new bugs appear.
Idle cabinet demo screens now explain ravens, field basics, upgrades, and armory/loadout concepts.
Stage flow now waits for active ravens and field powerups to clear before showing stage-clear.
A quick pooling pass now reuses title-screen ravens and high-churn gameplay effects for better long-session performance.
remainingValidation: Full release QA checklist still needs an end-to-end pass.
SEO and web kit/release shell metadata need to be completed before public sharing.
Final balance sign-off across the full 9-stage arc still needs continued play-session confirmation.
Real mobile/touch-device validation and public GitHub Pages deployment validation remain open.

knownUnfinishedAreas: SEO and web kit/release shell polish are still required: page title/description, Open Graph/Twitter metadata, favicon/app icons, web manifest, social preview image, canonical/public URL decisions, and any iOS/mobile web app tags.
Final browser/manual QA checklist has not been executed end to end.
Remote GitHub Actions and public GitHub Pages deployment have not been validated from GitHub.
Recent playtests are positive, but final balance sign-off across the full 9-stage arc is still required before public release.
Final original production visuals remain optional release polish: raven variants, boss sprite work, richer UI cabinet art, and optional recorded audio.
Mobile tuning was implemented in code but still needs real touch-device validation.

nextTeamPrimaryFocus.theme: Public release preparation: SEO, web kit/release shell polish, final release QA, and GitHub Pages deployment validation after positive gameplay playtests.

priorityOrder.title: Complete SEO and web kit release shell
details: Update index.html and any public release assets with production-ready title, meta description, Open Graph/Twitter tags, favicon/app icons, web manifest, theme color, and mobile/iOS web app tags.
Create or add a social preview image that represents the actual game instead of a generic placeholder.
Confirm all release metadata works from the GitHub Pages project path and does not assume a root-domain deployment unless the final URL supports it.

priorityOrder.title: Complete release QA checklist
details: Run DOCS/RELEASE-QA-CHECKLIST.md end to end against a production preview or deployment candidate.
Confirm all menu, armory, records, options, credits, idle demo, pause, stage-clear, game-over, weapon, enemy, powerup, bonus round, boss, and performance-pooling flows.

priorityOrder.title: Finish final balance and mobile sign-off
details: Do not rework the between-stage or armory-forward flows unless a new bug appears; recent playtests say those flows are working well.
Use DOCS/BALANCE-NOTES.md and npm run balance:report as the numeric baseline.

Continue play-session confirmation for the staggered enemy reveal curve, Jackpot Alley payout, Stage 8/9 pressure, Wraith/Brute readability, boss health, powerup drop rates, stage length, rewards, unlock timing, and real mobile/touch-device feel.

priorityOrder.title: Validate deployment and public build
details: Run npm run release:verify locally before handing off a release candidate.
Confirm the remote Release Verification workflow is green on the release commit.
Run the manual Deploy GitHub Pages workflow from master and validate the published URL with fresh and returning local saves.

currentBacklogFromDocs: Complete SEO and web kit/release shell polish: title, description, Open Graph/Twitter metadata, favicon/app icons, web manifest, theme color, social preview image, and mobile/iOS web app tags.
Complete final economy and difficulty sign-off from real play sessions; latest playtests are positive but not full release QA.
Validate mobile-specific tuning on real touch devices.
Run remote GitHub Actions and manual GitHub Pages deploy workflow before public release.
Optional: finish or replace final production visuals such as raven variants, boss sprite work, richer UI cabinet art, and optional recorded music/SFX.

recommendedFirstSprint.slice: SEO, web kit, and final release QA
recommendedFirstSprint.reason: Gameplay and progression are now in release-candidate shape after the staggered enemy balance pass, idle cabinet demo screens, field-drain stage flow, and pooling optimization pass. The next risk is public-release readiness: web metadata, share previews, app icons/manifest, final QA, mobile validation, and deployment confidence.
acceptance: SEO and social metadata are production-ready and point at the intended public GitHub Pages URL.
Favicon/app icons, web manifest, theme color, and mobile/iOS web app tags are present and load from the deployed project path.
Full release QA checklist is completed against a production preview or deployment candidate.
Manual play sessions continue to confirm the slower enemy introduction curve, Stage 7 bonus pacing, Stage 8/9 pressure, new enemy readability, field-drain stage endings, and long-session performance.
Real touch-device validation confirms HUD density, touch hit radius, stage-clear screens, idle demo readability, and game-over armory recommendations.
DOCS/BALANCE-NOTES.md is updated if tuning changes materially.

architecturePointers.runtime: src/main.ts creates Phaser game with BootScene, AttractScene, and GameScene.
architecturePointers.gameplayScene: src/game/scenes/GameScene.ts owns active run spawning, input, hit resolution, stage flow, boss flow, background set dressing, effects, and HUD dispatch.
architecturePointers.menuScene: src/game/scenes/AttractScene.ts owns attract screen state, idle cabinet demo rotation, armory/records/options/credits commands, save mutations, menu music, and pooled title-screen ravens.
architecturePointers.uiLayer: src/ui/app.ts renders DOM overlays for attract, armory, records, options, HUD, stage clear, pause, and game over. src/ui/events.ts is the scene/UI command bridge.
architecturePointers.releaseShell: index.html and public/ are the primary places for SEO, Open Graph/Twitter metadata, favicon/app icons, web manifest, social preview images, and mobile web app tags.
architecturePointers.data: src/game/data/stages.ts, enemies.ts, weapons.ts, upgrades.ts, tuning.ts are the main data/tuning entry points.
architecturePointers.save: src/game/save.ts normalizes localStorage save data and applies purchases/rewards.
architecturePointers.balance: scripts/report-balance.mjs reads live TypeScript data and estimates stage pacing, rewards, and economy costs.
architecturePointers.releaseChecks: scripts/check-release.mjs validates dist files. scripts/smoke-dist.mjs serves dist from /KnotzBirdShot/ and fetches linked assets.

recentManualPlaytestSignals: General playtests have been working well.
The game-over push to Armory now makes sense.
manualValidationStillRequired: SEO and web kit/release shell validation
Manual GitHub Pages deployment through the Deploy GitHub Pages workflow_dispatch event

recommendedSubagents.name: SEO And Web Kit Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Production title/description, Open Graph/Twitter metadata, favicon/app icons, web manifest, social preview image, theme color, iOS/mobile web app tags, and deployed path validation.

recommendedSubagents.name: Mobile Performance Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Real-device touch feel, HUD density, performance caps, and mobile readability.

recommendedSubagents.name: Run Flow And Progression Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Protect the now-validated between-stage UI, field-drain stage endings, idle cabinet demo, and game-over-to-armory flow while checking save-safe progression surfaces.

recommendedSubagents.name: Combat Feel And Weapon FX Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Gun identity, Assist Chip tuning, projectile/tracer feedback, shotgun and multibullet effects, cooldown readability.

recommendedSubagents.name: Release QA And Deployment Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Release gates, final QA checklist tracking, GitHub Pages Actions deployment, published-build validation, fresh-save and returning-save checks, and release notes.

githubPagesDeploymentNote.why: The classic Pages branch/folder source can show a blank white screen after the Vite migration because it may serve stale or mismatched static output. The Actions deployment uploads the freshly built dist artifact.

implementationGuidance: Keep gameplay rules and tunables in data/system modules where possible instead of burying values in scene code.
Use DOM overlays for text-heavy UI such as between-stage summaries, armory prompts, upgrade recommendations, and end-game progression prompts.
Use Phaser graphics/sprites for playfield effects, projectile traces, impact bursts, gun-driven reticle animation, and stage/boss presentation.
Treat SEO and web kit work as release work: validate metadata, icons, manifest, and share previews from the deployed GitHub Pages path, not only from local dev.
When save shape changes, update normalization in src/game/save.ts and test returning-save behavior manually.
After content or tuning changes, rerun npm run balance:report and update DOCS/BALANCE-NOTES.md if the baseline changes materially.
After runtime, asset, or release-script changes, run npm run release:verify before handoff.
openDesignQuestions: Should Assist Chips stay as one-installed-at-a-time cabinet hardware for v1, or should later releases add multiple chip slots?
Should the deterministic Jackpot Alley bonus round remain the v1 bonus model, or should performance-triggered bonus rounds wait for post-v1?
What is the desired full-armory completion time for an average player?
